title: Spotlight on Indonesian Thermal Coal Shipments
section_path: Spotlight on Indonesian Thermal Coal Shipments
section_path_text: Spotlight on Indonesian Thermal Coal Shipments
summary:
Main
WEEKLY DRY MARKET MONITOR Spot Rates | Supply-Demand Trends | Port Congestion Overview
Week 26, 2025 | Date: June 26, 2025
Chart of the Week: Spotlight on Indonesian Thermal Coal Shipments
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2025-06-27_spotlight-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-shipments_img_unnamed_b50df50c3246.png
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/amic-drybulkflows-downloadable/2r7gxx/506106936/h/1cCf1HbH259FoyN5B7cgGryYlEgqZv4_op4HzRuDMm4
This week's Market Monitor highlights a notable shift in thermal coal trade flows, particularly in the evolving import strategies of China and India. According to the latest dry bulk flow data, Indonesia continues to account for 48% of global thermal coal exports, with Taboneo and Muara Berau remaining the most active origin terminals. However, Chinese and Indian buyers are increasingly turning to higher-grade coal alternatives from countries such as Australia and Russia.

This week's Market Monitor highlights a notable shift in thermal coal trade flows, particularly in the evolving import strategies of China and India. According to the latest dry bulk flow data, Indonesia continues to account for 48% of global thermal coal exports, with Taboneo and Muara Berau remaining the most active origin terminals.

children.title: Tonne Days Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (8).png | assets/2025-06-27_spotlight-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-shipments_img_unnamed28829_cddd7c5d4678.png
Two weeks ago, our weekly market monitor highlighted the growth in iron ore tonne-days, which underpinned a stronger performance in the Baltic Capesize Index. Conversely, the coal segment faces pressure; Panamax and Supramax earnings are challenged by a continuous decline in tonne-day growth, consistently staying below the levels seen in the past two years.

children.title: SECTION 4/ WAYPOINTS
children.summary:
Strait of Hormuz
Image reference: unnamed (9).png | assets/2025-06-27_spotlight-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-shipments_img_unnamed28929_88a49af8b712.png
Following the peaceful agreement between Iran and Israel, maritime activity through the Strait of Hormuz is expected to remain normalized. However, a degree of risk persists for shipowners due to ongoing security concerns in the region.
